sourceMovie.title: Asteroid City
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Asteroid City (2023) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Wes Anderson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The King of Comedy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Asteroid City clicked for you, The King of Comedy (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Asteroid City clicked for you, Ed Wood (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Asteroid City?
faq.answer: Try The King of Comedy, Ed Wood, My Girl—each shares DNA with Asteroid City while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Asteroid City?
faq.answer: Asteroid City (2023) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Asteroid City worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
